{"id":15,"date":"2022-08-15T10:24:20","date_gmt":"2022-08-15T10:24:20","guid":{"rendered":"https://www.flickr.org//?page_id=15"},"modified":"2026-01-09T13:54:46","modified_gmt":"2026-01-09T13:54:46","slug":"home","status":"publish","type":"page","link":"https://www.flickr.org/","title":{"rendered":"Home"},"content":{"rendered":"

The Flickr Foundation

From family photos to historic cultural archives, we are developing ethical tools to keep digital legacies alive and accessible.

Images from Flickr + Flickr Commons

Learn More About Us
Flickr Commons

Bringing Archives to Life

Our flagship program, Flickr Commons, has helped over 100 institutions like NASA and the Library of Congress share almost two million photographs with the world, sparking curiosity, uncovering stories, and keeping history alive.

Explore Flickr CommonsBecome a Commons Member
Image from The Smithsonian Institute on Flickr Commons
Content Mobility

Data Lifeboat is live!

A photo on Flickr isn’t just a photo.
It comes with comments, connections, and context that tell a richer story.

We’ve created a tool to archive the whole story of Flickr photos.

Make a Flickr archiveLearn more

Support our work

The Flickr Foundation is proudly non-profit.

Together we’re building communities and systems to advance social media archiving, ensuring our shared digital heritage is preserved safely regardless of corporations’ lifespans.

Looking for a way to support our work?

Donate

Your help ensures our digital legacies live on.

Make a donation

Collaborate

We are always looking to build our community.

Get involved

Share our work

Follow us on social media and help spread the word.

Follow us
Blog

The Latest

‘Threading Ephemeral’: Oreoluwa’s closing reflections on the Fellowship

As Oreoluwa's Fellowship project draws to a close, they share their concluding artistic output and critical reflections on image-making

Read more

The State of Social Media Archiving

Read our market research on how social media platforms allow their users to download their stuff.

Read the report

2026: Simplifying Our Strategy

We're making adjustments to our programming and team in the coming months to weather the current storm affecting nonprofits across the US and more broadly.

Learn more about our plans
